example-moving-mesh-stress.title: Moving Mesh Stress
example-moving-mesh-stress.description: 900 individually-animated meshes with per-frame quaternion updates and world matrix recalculations.

example-static-mesh-stress.title: Static Mesh Stress
example-static-mesh-stress.description: 900 static meshes with no per-frame updates — measures pure rendering overhead.

example-texture.title: Texture
example-texture.description: A textured spinning box demonstrating user-provided Texture, UV coordinates, and texture sampling.

example-instanced-mesh.title: Instanced Mesh
example-instanced-mesh.description: 30 instanced boxes with per-instance transforms and colours via InstancedBufferAttributeNode.

example-instancing-storage-buffer.title: Instancing with Storage Buffer
example-instancing-storage-buffer.description: 35 instanced boxes using storage buffers (mat4x4f transforms + vec4f colours) indexed by instance_index, combined with an instancedBufferAttribute for per-instance spin offset.

example-compute-particles.title: Compute Particles
example-compute-particles.description: 8192 GPU-simulated particles updated each frame by a @compute shader (renderer.compute) and rendered as instanced quads — no CPU readback.

example-indirect-batched.title: Indirect Batched
example-indirect-batched.description: CPU-driven indirect rendering: one merged geometry holds box and sphere data; two IndirectBuffers select sub-ranges via firstIndex + instanceCount. A slider moves the box/sphere split at runtime.

example-indirect-compute.title: Indirect Compute
example-indirect-compute.description: GPU-driven indirect rendering: a compute shader writes instanceCount directly into the indirect draw buffer each frame via IndirectBuffer.asStorageNode(), culling instances by distance — no CPU readback.

example-mrt.title: MRT (Multiple Render Targets)
example-mrt.description: Multiple Render Targets (MRT): renders a sphere to 4 targets (output, normal, diffuse, emissive) and composites them side-by-side for visualization.

example-raging-sea.title: Raging Sea
example-raging-sea.description: Animated ocean surface using sinusoidal large waves and multi-octave value noise small waves, with finite-difference normals and emissive wave crests — all computed on the GPU via vertex shader uniforms.

example-uniforms.title: Uniforms
example-uniforms.description: Demonstrates two uniform patterns: inline uniforms (shared across meshes) and name-based uniforms (per-material values resolved at render time).

example-storage.title: Storage Buffers
example-storage.description: Demonstrates two storage buffer patterns: inline storage (buffer passed directly) and name-based storage (resolved from geometry.buffers at render time).

example-voxels.title: Voxels
example-voxels.description: Procedurally generated voxel landscape using layered simplex noise, with culled meshing per chunk and per-vertex ambient occlusion computed on the CPU.

example-voxels-batched.title: Voxels Batched
example-voxels-batched.description: Batched voxel renderer with slab-allocated merged geometry, GPU frustum culling via compute shader, indirect draws, render distance management, and realtime voxel editing.
